Reading Stats from Leaderboards

To read stats for one or more players, your title calls the XOnlineStatRead and XOnlineStatReadGetResult functions. The XOnlineStatRead function begins the asynchronous task of retrieving statistical information. It passes a handle back to your title through its phTask parameter. To continue work on this task, your title must call the XOnlineTaskContinue function. When XOnlineTaskContinue indicates that the task is complete, your title calls XOnlineStatReadGetResult to obtain the results of the task. Finally, close the task by calling XOnlineTaskClose.

The XOnlineStatRead function takes an array of XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structures that specify the players for whom to obtain stats. The dwLeaderBoardID member of each XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structure specifies the leaderboard from which to obtain the stats. Because the leaderboard is specified on a per-player basis, a single call to XOnlineStatRead can be used to obtain stats from users on multiple leaderboards. The pStats member of each XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structure points to an array of XONLINE_STAT structures that specify which stats to retrieve.

Use the XONLINE_STAT structures to specify both title-defined stats and service-defined stats such as rating, rank, and nickname. Because the stats to retrieve are specified on a per-player basis, a different set of stats can be obtained for each player. XOnlineStatRead creates an asynchronous task to retrieve the stats from the stats service. When this task completes, call XOnlineStatReadGetResult to obtain the stats. The title must pass XOnlineStatReadGetResult the same set of XONLINE_STAT_SPEC and XONLINE_STAT structures that the title passed to XOnlineStatRead.

The XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate and XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerateGetResults functions retrieve an ordered contiguous listing of players from a specified leaderboard. As with XOnlineStatRead and XOnlineStatReadGetResult, your program must call XOnlineTaskContinue and XOnlineTaskClose to perform this task. The listing retrieved by XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate and XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerateGetResultsis sorted by player ranking. Unlike the listing from the XOnlineStatRead and XOnlineStatReadGetResultfunctions, the XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate and XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerateGetResults functions retrieve players and their stats from only a single leaderboard per call. Also, the set of stats retrieved is the same for each player.

The dwPageSize parameter for XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate specifies the number of players to retrieve. The title can specify which players to retrieve in one of two ways. The first is to specify the leaderboard entry at which to begin the enumeration. Specify the entry in the dwPageStart parameter for XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate. For example, to start the enumeration at the top of the leaderboard, specify a value of one. A second way to specify the players to retrieve is to specify a player who should be located in the middle of the list. Specify the player by passing the XUID structure for the player as the pxuidPagePivot parameter. XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate attempts to retrieve an equal number of players above and below the specified pivot player. A title can request no more than 100 players with one invocation of XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate.

The XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerateGetResults function retrieves the leaderboard entries requested by XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate. The pUsers parameter points to a buffer that receives an array of XONLINE_STAT_USER structures that correspond to the players retrieved. The pStats parameter points to a buffer that receives an array of XONLINE_STAT structures containing the stats for these players. The pdwReturnedResults parameter receives the number of players retrieved, and the pdwLeaderboardSize parameter receives the total number of players listed on the leaderboard.
